core_mechanism: self-consistent mean-field (fixed-point) reduction that replaces an all-to-all many-body interaction with an effective single-body field determined self-consistently from the system's own averaged/measured state, becoming exact in the large-system limit
brief_reason: The abstract emulates fully-connected transverse interactions by a self-consistent transverse field fixed from transverse-basis measurements, yielding identical dynamics in the large-system limit — the hallmark of a self-consistent mean-field closure of an all-to-all coupling.
